author: jsign
content:
Cool. Remember as said in my original post -- this is filled with geth, so if geth isn't spec compliant there is a risk the `statelessInputBytes` aren't correct in some shape or form. 
But yeah, if you can dig a bit on why we might have a clue and find the reason and be able to fix it 🙏 (just don't jump to assume is a bug in your code)

author: jsign
content:
I actually need to find some time into including Geth into the Hive-setup run to actually see if passes all executionWitness construction tests... that might be a good ROI for next week (for everyone here...).
I'll first wait for your digging results to calibrate the urgency of this. (Best case for me is taht is a bug in your codebase 😄 )

author: omerfirmak
content:
Running them as blockchain test fixtures is fine, but running them using statelessInputBytes yieds the errors below (all related to witnesses). So there is a possibility the issue is on geth's side.

```       **err: error.SubTrieNotAvailable**
       compute/scenario/test_transaction_types.py::test_ether_transfers[fork_Amsterdam-blockchain_test-warm_access_False-empty_account-transfer_amount_0-case_id_a_to_diff_acc-benchmark-gas-value_60M]
       **err: error.SubTrieNotAvailable**
       compute/scenario/test_transaction_types.py::test_ether_transfers[fork_Amsterdam-blockchain_test-warm_access_True-empty_account-transfer_amount_0-case_id_a_to_diff_acc-benchmark-gas-value_60M]
       **err: error.SubTrieNotAvailable**
       compute/instruction/test_account_query.py::test_ext_account_query_cold[fork_Amsterdam-blockchain_test-absent_accounts_True-opcode_BALANCE-benchmark-gas-value_60M]
       **err: error.SubTrieNotAvailable**
       compute/instruction/test_system.py::test_contract_calling_many_addresses[fork_Amsterdam-blockchain_test-access_warm_False-opcode_CALL-transfer_amount_0-benchmark-gas-value_60M]
       **err: error.SubTrieNotAvailable**
       compute/instruction/test_system.py::test_contract_calling_many_addresses[fork_Amsterdam-blockchain_test-access_warm_False-opcode_CALLCODE-transfer_amount_0-benchmark-gas-value_60M]
       **err: error.SubTrieNotAvailable**
       compute/instruction/test_system.py::test_contract_calling_many_addresses[fork_Amsterdam-blockchain_test-access_warm_False-opcode_CALLCODE-transfer_amount_1-benchmark-gas-value_60M]
       **err: error.InvalidAncestors**
       compute/instruction/test_block_context.py::test_blockhash[fork_Amsterdam-blockchain_test-block_1-benchmark-gas-value_60M]
        **err: error.InvalidAncestors**
       compute/instruction/test_block_context.py::test_blockhash[fork_Amsterdam-blockchain_test-random-benchmark-gas-value_60M]```

author: omerfirmak
content: looks like geth emits ancestor header chain in the wrong order
